methods.method_name: Statistical Analysis of Buzzard Foraging Behavior
methods.description: This method involves statistical procedures to analyze the foraging behavior of buzzards on different types of dough, using generalized linear models (GLM) and Chi-square tests to determine preferences and interactions between olfaction and vision.

steps.description: We applied a generalized linear model (GLM) using the GENMOD procedure in SAS 9.4 to model the probabilities of foraging first on the original yellow pollen dough, the black dough with all three ingredients, and the darker dough in all three experiments.
steps.searchable_summary: apply GLM using SAS GENMOD to model foraging probabilities

steps.description: We used a Chi-square test with one degree of freedom to examine if the frequencies of these events were biased away from a 50:50 probability.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Chi-square test to check for bias in foraging events

steps.description: We used a GLM to model the probability of feeding first on the pollen-containing dough with the dough's color as the independent variable.
steps.searchable_summary: model feeding probability with GLM using dough color as variable

steps.description: We modeled the visitation rate with a GLM, with the proportion of yellow doughs provided in each respective experiment as the independent variable.
steps.searchable_summary: model visitation rate with GLM using proportion of yellow doughs

methods.description: This method describes the process of isolating genomic DNA from blood samples, amplifying olfactory receptor genes using PCR, and analyzing the sequences to identify functional and non-functional genes.

steps.description: Blood samples collected during the study were used for genomic DNA isolation with a commercially available kit.
steps.searchable_summary: isolate genomic DNA from blood samples using a commercial kit

steps.description: We used degenerated primer combinations to amplify the partial γ-c and non-γ-c OR genes in PCR.
steps.searchable_summary: amplify OR genes using PCR with degenerated primers

steps.description: PCR products were separated in agarose gels before ligation to a cloning vector.
steps.searchable_summary: separate PCR products in agarose gels and ligate to cloning vector

steps.description: Plasmids were purified from transformed colonies using a commercial kit, and sequencing was conducted.
Electropherograms were inspected to delete PCR primer sequences, and sequences were examined using BLASTX to eliminate non-vertebrate OR genes.
steps.searchable_summary: inspect electropherograms and use BLASTX to filter sequences

steps.description: Sequences were checked for non-functional OR genes using open reading frame finder and Molecular Toolkit.
steps.searchable_summary: check sequences for stop codons using ORF finder and Molecular Toolkit

steps.description: Estimate the total number of OR genes in the genome using established procedures.
steps.searchable_summary: estimate total OR genes using established procedures
